div.p: O(N) Linear Growth Each query computes distance to ALL N vectors. Computation cost doubles as dataset doubles.
HNSW Logic: O(log N) vs O(N) Complexity.

Brute-force KNN has linear complexity O(N) because every query must calculate the distance to every single stored vector. While functional for small sets, this behavior creates a hard performance ceiling as datasets reach 1M+ vectors.

HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small Worlds) solves this by building a graph of vectors where connections prioritize proximity. Traversal occurs through hierarchical layers, reaching the neighborhood of the result in O(log N) steps.

p.em: At 1M vectors, you must compute 768 million float operations per query.
